title: Choose metrics that track the real goal
description: Select metrics that move only when the user-facing quality moves, and reject proxies a system can game without improving.
body: Use when deciding how to score a system. Do not optimize a convenient proxy (exact-match, token overlap) when the goal is semantic correctness, and avoid a metric that can rise while real quality falls. Actions: state the user-facing goal, pick metrics that demonstrably correlate with it, and pair an automatic metric with a human-labeled check on a sample. Validation signals include a correlation check between the metric and human judgments on the same cases. Risk boundary: optimizing a gameable proxy ships a regression that the metric hides; if a metric and human judgment diverge, trust the human and flag the metric.
